part.text:
Build still green. Done.

**What changed**
- `src/stations.ts` — 5-station assembly data (Stamp → Weld → Paint → Inspect → Pack) + caption helpers.
- `src/AssemblyScroll.tsx` — scroll-driven view. A `520vh` track + `100dvh` sticky stage. One passive `scroll` listener schedules a single `requestAnimationFrame`; the callback reads the track's bounding box and writes `translate`/`width`/`opacity`/`data-stage` **directly to DOM refs** — zero `setState` on the scroll path, so no React re-render loops. Part travels 5%→95% along an animated conveyor; each tool-arm descends, glows, and sparks when the part is under it; the workpiece cumulatively evolves (raw → stamped → riveted → painted → checked → crated).
- `src/AssemblyStatic.tsx` — reduced-motion view. No scroll hijack, no continuous motion: a step-through workpiece (buttons + arrow-friendly roadmap pills) plus a full card per station. Same data, fully readable.
- `src/App.tsx` — header, hero, mode switch (defaults to OS `prefers-reduced-motion`, persists to `localStorage`), details section, footer.
- `src/style.css`, `src/main.tsx`, `index.html` — styling, entry, title.

**Reduced-motion path:** toggle (or OS setting) swaps out the sticky scroll stage entirely for the static stepper; a `prefers-reduced-motion` guard also pauses ambient CSS animations as defense-in-depth.

**Verification:** `npm run build` (runs `tsc --noEmit` + `vite build`) — passes, 1703 modules, no type or transform errors. Also confirmed the dev server boots and serves HTTP 200 and all lucide icon imports resolve.
